page.title: Marbella Boat Rental in November: Weather, Sea & Charter Tips

data.meta_description: Plan a Marbella boat rental in November with our weather, sea & charter tips. Average 18°C, calm seas, & lower rates at Puerto Banús. Book your trip today.
data.summary: Discover what to expect from a Marbella boat rental in November: mild weather, calm Mediterranean seas, and off-peak charter deals. Get practical tips for a successful winter cruise from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Average November air temperature in Marbella: 18°C (64°F).
Sea temperature: around 17°C (63°F) – wetsuit or warm jacket recommended.
Charter rates are lower than summer peak – e.g., Astondoa 40 from €749/2h.
Puerto Banús remains open; fewer crowds mean easier berthing.
Sunset around 18:15 – plan half-day (4h) or shorter charters.
Rainfall: ~70 mm across the month, mostly short showers.
North-westerly winds (Terral) possible – check forecast before sailing.

faq.q: Can I swim from a boat in Marbella in November?
faq.a: Yes, but the sea temperature averages 17°C. Most swimmers use a shorty wetsuit or just a quick dip. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are sheltered spots where water is slightly calmer.

faq.q: What is the best time of day for a November boat trip?
faq.a: Midday to late afternoon (12:00–16:00) offers the warmest air temperature and best light. Sunset is around 18:15, so a 4-hour charter from 12:00 works well.

faq.q: Are boat rentals cheaper in November?
faq.a: Yes, many boats have reduced rates. For example, our Astondoa 40 starts at €749 for 2 hours, and the Azimut 39 at the same price. Longer charters are also negotiable – contact us via WhatsApp for custom quotes.

faq.q: Do you still offer drinks and snacks on winter charters?
faq.a: Absolutely. Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks – all year round. Just let us know if you have dietary preferences.

faq.q: Which boats are best for a November charter?
faq.a: Enclosed flybridge models like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 keep you warm. Open boats like the Bandido or Speedboat are fine on calm days but bring a jacket. Our full fleet is available from Puerto Banús.

faq.q: What is the wind like in Marbella in November?
faq.a: Prevailing winds are light to moderate (5–15 knots) from the west or northwest. The Terral wind can bring warmer, dry gusts but rarely exceeds 20 knots. We always monitor conditions and cancel if unsafe – your deposit is fully refundable.

faq.q: Can I see dolphins in November?
faq.a: Yes, common dolphins and bottlenose dolphins are spotted year-round off the Marbella coast. November often sees them closer to shore near Cabopino and the Río Verde estuary.

faq.q: Do you operate on Christmas or New Year?
faq.a: We operate through November and into December, but are closed on 25 December and 1 January. Private charters on other days are possible – book early via WhatsApp.

data.body_html:
Thinking about a Marbella boat rental in November? The Costa del Sol offers mild winter days, calm seas, and far fewer tourists than summer. With average air temperatures of 18°C and sea water around 17°C, November is a surprisingly pleasant month for a coastal cruise. Here’s everything you need to know about Marbella boat rental weather November – plus practical tips for a smooth charter from Puerto Banús.

November Weather in Marbella: What to Expect

Marbella enjoys a Mediterranean microclimate. November brings daytime highs of around 20°C and lows of 12°C. Rain is possible – typically short showers totalling about 70 mm for the month – but sunny spells dominate. The sea temperature averages 17°C, so a wetsuit is recommended if you plan to swim. Winds are generally light (5–15 knots) from the west or northwest, though the occasional Terral wind can bring warmer, dry gusts. Always check the 48-hour forecast before your trip.

Best Boats for a November Charter

Enclosed or semi-enclosed boats are ideal for cooler days. Our Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a heated saloon and flybridge cover. The Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) has a spacious cockpit with wind protection. For smaller groups, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is affordable at €749 for 2 hours. Open boats like the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax) are fine on calm days – just bring a warm jacket. All depart from Puerto Banús.

Charter Rates & Off-Peak Deals

November is off-peak, so rates are lower than summer. Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for 8 hours – skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA included. Tier B (Mangusta 80 variants) are €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter with a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request – message us on WhatsApp for custom quotes. No hidden fees.

Where to Cruise: November Route Ideas

With shorter daylight (sunset ~18:15), half-day charters (4 hours) work best. Head west to Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés – sheltered coves with calm water. Eastwards, Cabopino offers a quiet anchorage near the dunes. For a longer trip, cruise to Estepona (10 NM) or Sotogrande (25 NM) – both have marinas open year-round. The view of La Concha mountain from the sea is stunning in winter light.

What’s Included in Your November Charter

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. Insurance and safety equipment are covered. The 21% Spanish IVA is already in the price. You can bring your own food or arrange catering – just let us know. The boat is heated if needed, and blankets are available on request.

Safety & Sea Conditions

November seas are generally calm, but we monitor wind and swell closely. We never sail in unsafe conditions – if we cancel due to weather, your deposit is fully refundable. The Mediterranean rarely sees large waves in November (typically <1m swell). However, the Terral wind can create choppy conditions near the coast – we’ll advise you on the best route. All our boats carry life jackets, flares, and first-aid kits.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I swim from a boat in Marbella in November?

Yes, but the sea temperature averages 17°C. Most swimmers use a shorty wetsuit or just a quick dip. Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are sheltered spots where water is slightly calmer.

What is the best time of day for a November boat trip?

Midday to late afternoon (12:00–16:00) offers the warmest air temperature and best light. Sunset is around 18:15, so a 4-hour charter from 12:00 works well.

Are boat rentals cheaper in November?

Yes, many boats have reduced rates. For example, our Astondoa 40 starts at €749 for 2 hours, and the Azimut 39 at the same price. Longer charters are also negotiable – contact us via WhatsApp for custom quotes.

Do you still offer drinks and snacks on winter charters?

Absolutely. Every charter includes water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks – all year round. Just let us know if you have dietary preferences.

Which boats are best for a November charter?

Enclosed flybridge models like the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 keep you warm. Open boats like the Bandido or Speedboat are fine on calm days but bring a jacket. Our full fleet is available from Puerto Banús.

What is the wind like in Marbella in November?

Prevailing winds are light to moderate (5–15 knots) from the west or northwest. The Terral wind can bring warmer, dry gusts but rarely exceeds 20 knots. We always monitor conditions and cancel if unsafe – your deposit is fully refundable.

Can I see dolphins in November?

Yes, common dolphins and bottlenose dolphins are spotted year-round off the Marbella coast. November often sees them closer to shore near Cabopino and the Río Verde estuary.

Do you operate on Christmas or New Year?

We operate through November and into December, but are closed on 25 December and 1 January. Private charters on other days are possible – book early via WhatsApp.

Ready to experience a Marbella boat rental in November? Explore our full fleet and book your winter escape from Puerto Banús today.
